flashcards.q: When two lines intersect, how many angles are formed?
flashcards.a: 4 angles — two pairs of vertically opposite angles and two pairs of linear pairs.

flashcards.q: What is a linear pair?
flashcards.a: Two adjacent angles formed at the intersection of two lines, together forming a straight angle. A linear pair always sums to 180°.

flashcards.q: What are vertically opposite angles?
flashcards.a: Angles formed directly across from each other at an intersection point. They are always equal to each other.

flashcards.q: Can two straight lines intersect at more than one point?
flashcards.a: No. Two straight lines can intersect at most at one point.

flashcards.q: If ∠a = 65° at an intersection, what are the other three angles?
flashcards.a: ∠b = 115° (linear pair), ∠c = 65° (vertically opposite), ∠d = 115° (vertically opposite to ∠b).

flashcards.q: Define perpendicular lines.
flashcards.a: Two lines that intersect at right angles (90°). All four angles formed at the intersection are 90°.

flashcards.q: How are perpendicular lines marked in a geometry figure?
flashcards.a: With a small square symbol (□) at the point of intersection.

flashcards.q: Define parallel lines.
flashcards.a: Two lines on the same plane that never intersect, however far extended. They maintain a constant perpendicular distance.

flashcards.q: How are parallel lines marked in a geometry figure?
flashcards.a: With matching single arrow (>) on each line. A second set uses double arrows (>>). Written as l ∥ m.

flashcards.q: Must parallel lines be on the same plane?
flashcards.a: Yes. Two lines on different planes that never meet are called skew lines — NOT parallel.

flashcards.q: What is a transversal?
flashcards.a: A line that intersects two other lines at distinct points, forming 8 angles — 4 at each intersection.

flashcards.q: How many distinct angle measures can exist among the 8 angles formed by a transversal?
flashcards.a: At most 4 distinct values — because vertically opposite pairs at each intersection are equal.

flashcards.q: Define corresponding angles.
flashcards.a: Pairs of angles formed by a transversal that occupy the same position (corner) at each intersection — one at line l, one at line m.

flashcards.q: Name all four pairs of corresponding angles formed by transversal t crossing lines l and m (angles 1–4 at l, 5–8 at m).
State the corresponding angles theorem (both directions).
flashcards.a: Corresponding angles are equal ↔ the lines are parallel. Equality is both necessary AND sufficient for parallelism.

flashcards.q: True or False: If corresponding angles are equal, the lines must be parallel.
flashcards.a: True. Equal corresponding angles guarantee parallelism — this works in both directions.

flashcards.q: Define alternate angles.
flashcards.a: A pair of angles formed by a transversal on opposite sides of the transversal, one at each intersection, lying between the two lines.

flashcards.q: When are alternate angles equal?
flashcards.a: When the two lines cut by the transversal are parallel. Alternate angles are always equal on parallel lines.

flashcards.q: How are alternate angles proved equal using other known facts?
flashcards.a: Step 1: Corresponding angles are equal (since lines are parallel). Step 2: Vertically opposite angles are equal. Combining: alternate angles are equal.

flashcards.q: What are co-interior angles (interior angles on the same side)?
flashcards.a: A pair of angles formed on the same side of the transversal, between the two parallel lines — one at each intersection.

flashcards.q: What is the sum of co-interior angles when lines are parallel?
flashcards.a: 180°. Co-interior angles always add up to 180° when the transversal crosses parallel lines.

flashcards.q: How is the co-interior angle rule proved?
flashcards.a: If ∠3 + ∠2 = 180° (linear pair) and ∠2 = ∠6 (corresponding angles), then ∠3 + ∠6 = 180°.

flashcards.q: If ∠6 = 135° on a transversal crossing parallel lines, what are ∠5 and ∠2?
flashcards.a: ∠5 = 45° (linear pair with ∠6); ∠2 = 135° (corresponding to ∠6, lines are parallel).

flashcards.q: How do you use a set square and ruler to draw a parallel line through a point?
flashcards.a: Place set square along line l; hold ruler against it; slide the set square along the ruler until reaching point A; draw the line through A.

flashcards.q: Why does the set square method produce a parallel line?
flashcards.a: Both lines make the same angle with the set square edge (90° or another fixed angle). Equal corresponding angles → lines are parallel.

flashcards.q: Describe the paper-folding method to draw a parallel to line l through point A.
flashcards.a: Fold a line t perpendicular to l through A (t ⊥ l). Then fold a line m perpendicular to t through A (m ⊥ t). Since l and m are both ⊥ to t, their corresponding angles with t are both 90° → l ∥ m.

flashcards.q: After n horizontal folds of a square sheet, how many parallel lines are visible?
flashcards.a: 2ⁿ + 1 parallel lines (e.g., 1 fold → 3 lines; 2 folds → 5 lines; 3 folds → 9 lines).

flashcards.q: What is the relationship between a vertical fold and previous horizontal folds on a paper?
flashcards.a: The vertical fold creates a line perpendicular to all the horizontal fold lines.

flashcards.q: True or False: If two lines are cut by a transversal and co-interior angles sum to 180°, the lines are parallel.
flashcards.a: True. Co-interior angles summing to 180° is equivalent to the lines being parallel.

flashcards.q: Two lines are cut by a transversal. Alternate angles are 73° and 73°. Are the lines parallel?
flashcards.a: Yes. Equal alternate angles imply the lines are parallel.

flashcards.q: What is a 'proof' in mathematics as described in this chapter?
flashcards.a: A logical justification of a statement using reasoning alone — not measurement. Example: proving vertically opposite angles are equal using linear pair sums.

flashcards.q: In a parallelogram (AB ∥ CD, AD ∥ BC), co-interior angles between AB and CD sum to…?
flashcards.a: 180°. Any pair of co-interior angles formed by a transversal cutting parallel lines sums to 180°.

flashcards.q: What is the maximum number of distinct angle measures when a transversal crosses two PARALLEL lines?
flashcards.a: Only 2 distinct values — because on parallel lines, corresponding angles are also equal, reducing the 4 possible values to just 2.

flashcards.q: If co-interior angles are (3x + 10)° and (2x + 20)°, and lines are parallel, find x.
State one real-life example each of parallel lines and perpendicular lines.
flashcards.a: Parallel: ruled lines on a notebook page (or railway tracks). Perpendicular: adjacent edges of a square sheet of paper (or a wall meeting the floor).
